plainEnglish: Changing a form control's value—selecting a radio, picking from a dropdown, typing—must not automatically cause a change of context unless you've warned the user first. Don't auto-submit a form when a select changes; use an explicit submit button instead.
whyItMatters: Screen reader and keyboard users are thrown off when selecting a dropdown option auto-submits or navigates before they've finished, with no warning.
quickCheck: Change each form control's value and confirm nothing auto-submits or navigates unless the user was clearly warned in advance.
commonMistakes: Auto-submitting or navigating in a select's onChange (a 'jump to' menu) with no warning or Go button.
Reloading the page when a checkbox or radio changes.
Advancing a wizard step the instant an option is selected.

codeExamples.label: Selection plus an explicit Go button
codeExamples.code:
<select value={page} onChange={(e) => setPage(e.target.value)}>{/* options */}</select>
<button onClick={() => navigate(page)}>Go</button>

codeExamples.label: Selection navigates with no warning
codeExamples.code:
{/* Navigates the instant a different option is chosen */}
<select onChange={(e) => navigate(e.target.value)}>{/* options */}</select>
